skillName: Algebraic notation and basic collecting like terms
misconceptionSummary: Students can copy notation but do not understand why algebra is written in a compact form.

visuals.ruleText: Algebra uses compact notation to show structure clearly
visuals.subText: Shorter notation helps us see multiplication, repetition and pattern

steps.narration: Algebraic notation is designed to be compact. We shorten repeated structures so the pattern becomes easier to see.

lines.text: The 4 tells us how many x's there are

steps.narration: A coefficient shows how many of a variable we have. Four x means four copies of x added together.
Powers count repeated multiplication. The power tells you how many equal factors of the variable are being multiplied together.

lines.text: The variable part stays the same

steps.narration: This notation also explains collecting like terms. In 2y plus 3y, the y stays the same and the coefficients combine to make 5y.

visuals.expression: Coefficients count repeated addition, powers count repeated multiplication
visuals.label: Algebraic notation shows the structure of the maths

steps.narration: Algebraic notation is not just a shortcut. It tells us exactly what kind of repetition is happening.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: seeing notation as arbitrary symbols. In fact, the notation carries meaning: coefficients count copies and powers count repeated multiplication.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: The symbols are not random. They tell you how the algebra is built.
